# Series Overview

The one-page answer to "what is this universe?" Fill it in before anything else — every other file assumes these decisions have been made.

---

## Core Concept

[Two or three sentences. Who are the protagonists, what do they do, what makes this universe worth returning to story after story?]

## Target Audience

[Who is this for? What do they read now? What's the reading experience — a crushable weekend read, a slow epic, an episodic serial?]

## Format

- **Story length:** [e.g., short novels of 40–60k words; novellas; episodic serials]
- **Series shape:** [e.g., six-book arc with one focal character per book; open-ended episodic; trilogy]
- **Structure within a story:** [e.g., four-part structure mapped to structural beats; three acts; case-of-the-week]
- **Timeline:** [How much in-world time does each story cover? How much passes between stories?]

## Setting

[Where and when. Be specific enough that a scene can be placed and checked — real geography or invented, the canon library's `locations/index.md` will hold the hard rules either way.]

## Series Guidelines

- [What every story in this universe must include]
- [What no story in this universe may do — cross-reference the red lines in `ai_instructions.md` §8]
- [How standalone each story must be — can a reader start at book 3?]
